rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Objection, Or, In The Alternative, File Cross-Petition
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Objector Jennifer Sam's Motion for Leave to Amend Objection, or in the Alternative, File Cross-Petition, is GRANTED. Objector shall file the Amended Objection within 5 days of entry of this Order. Appearances at the hearing are required to discuss the pending trial. Judicial policy favors resolution of all disputed matters between the parties in the same lawsuit, and the Court finds neither of Petitioner's grounds to deny the motion persuasive. Trustee George Koyuly served the "Notification by Trustee Pursuant to California Probate Code Section 16061.7," notifying recipients of the administration of the Sam Trust "as amended and restated in its entirety on March 18, 2021." Thus, the Notice informed recipients of the administration of the 2021 Trust not the 2019 Trust, which is the focus of the new claims and allegations in the proposed Amended Objection. With respect to Petitioner's contention that the proposed Amended Objection fails to state a cause of action, those arguments are proper for demurrer or motion to strike. The Court does not consider the validity of the proposed amended pleading in deciding whether to grant leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup.Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048; Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 760 ["the better course of action would have been to allow [plaintiff] to amend the complaint and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings"].) The parties should come prepared to discuss a possible continuance of the September 20, 2023 trial date and counsels' availability in the coming months. In any event, the Court is inclined to open discovery for the limited purpose of investigating and preparing a defense to the new claims at trial. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE NOLE MARINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/18/2023 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 08/18/2023). The following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/21/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petitioner's last name was incorrectly stated on the order for probate. The order has not been corrected. 4) If the petitioner is requesting approval of her final account, the petition must be amended to include settlement of the final account in the caption as additional relief sought. 5) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 6) The first account period cannot exceed two years from the date of death. LRSF 14.34(A). 7) Financial account statements must be filed to verify the ending cash balance (LRSF 14.34(D)), together with a declaration re retention of the original statements required by LRSF 14.59(A). 8) A schedule of liabilities (Probate Code section 1063(g)) must be filed. An allegation must also be made as to whether there are outstanding bills due to a third party for preparing the account. LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(a)(iii). 9) Please itemize the $1,460 reimbursement to the petitioner reported as a disbursement. 10) If the petitioner is requesting reimbursement of $1,500 (advanced to open the estate bank account), the amount must be deducted from the fee base receipts. The statutory compensation and net cash distribution to the beneficiaries must be adjusted. 11) The $2,500 disbursement for bookkeeping/accounting services must be deducted from the petitioner's statutory compensation. LRSF 14.51. The statutory compensation and net cash distribution to the beneficiaries must be adjusted. An appearance may be required on 10/18/2023 if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SAUL TRUJILLO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/4/23 (the earliest available date). The following procedurlla issues on Accounting #1 (Exhibit A) to be cleared. Once the issues have been cleared, the examiner will review (the second period) Exhibit B. 1. Clarification on the ending date. It must set forth the date of death as alleged in petition. Listing 6/23/22(7/2/22) as ending date appears inappropriate. PC 2620(b). All schedules should be amended to set forth 7/2/22. 2. Clarification on the balance as of 5/27/22 in checking 6844. The balance amount on the statement differs from the schedule. Any discrepancy must be reconciled. 3. Receipts and Disbursement must mirror all deposits/withdrawals. Schedule A and C do not explain all transactions appearing on the attached bank statements. PC 1062. 4. Schedule G Liabilities shows multiple payments to various payees. The information must be reported on Accounting #2 (Exhibit B) Disbursements. 5. Ending balance of the account 6844 as of 7/2/22 to be listed. (See the above issue #1). 6. Beginning non-cash assets are not identical from those on I&A. PC 1061(a)(1) and (b). 7. Petition to set forth the vesting of the real property. I&A lists a parcel of real property; however, Petition does not report it as the deceased conservatee's assets. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HIROKO SALLY DY FOON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Sidney Foon, one of the decedent's two children having higher priority than Crystal Foon (one of the petitioners) and having equal priority with the other petitioner Stevan Foon, has not waived his priority under Probate Code section 8465(b). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
